creator: mooltw@gmail.com
text:
Hi ALL , 

I had two 384GB memory server (Cisco UCS-240M3 & Supermicro X9DRi-LN4F+)

When I using FreeBSD-9.1-RELEASE-amd64-bootonly.iso disc boot 
boot script will stop at <mount_root> .
Looks like USB CDROM can't find ... and USB keyboard can't type any key. 

console message is 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 
usbd_ctrl_transfer_setup: could not setup default USB transfer 


I try to booting from cdrom use FreeBSD-8.3-RELEASE-amd64-bootonly.iso is okay... Debian / Ubuntu is okay . 

Other way is.. plug out memory to 288GB , FreeBSD 9.1 boot is normal ...

So.. FreeBSD 8.3 on 384GB memory server is okay , but FreeBSD 9.1 is not.

Fix: 

At boot menu 

[2] escape to loader prompt


OK  set kern.maxbcache="64M" 
OK  boot
How-To-Repeat: 384GB memory
